commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=5b3acc1e3448fffe8e0602dc67233a85d841a2b9)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds persistent main-session restart-recovery attempt/quarantine fields, increments them at restart mark sites, quarantines over-budget rows, clears the budget on successful lifecycle completion, and adds focused tests plus reserved slot keys.

PR surface: Source +102, Tests +135. Total +237 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main selects running main-session rows with abortedLastRun=true and has no persisted cross-boot attempt ceiling, matching https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/95750. I did not run the full Telegram/systemd production loop in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review/contributor action is needed because missing real behavior proof cannot be supplied by automation and the code still needs the guard-order fix before merge.

target.title: fix(gateway): bound cross-boot restart-recovery with a persistent retry budget
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Closes #95750.

When a channel main-session turn wedges and a restart marks the entry `abortedLastRun: true`, the next boot's recovery pass calls back into the same resume path **unconditionally**. The in-process orchestrator (`MAX_RECOVERY_RETRIES = 3`) only bounds retries *within a single process invocation* — the counter is never persisted, so each fresh boot is a fresh retry budget. The result is a deterministic death loop until `systemd`'s `StartLimitBurst` is exhausted and an operator must intervene.

This adds a **persistent, cross-boot retry budget** to main-session restart recovery so a genuinely wedged session can no longer death-loop the gateway across reboots. A session that can't be safely resumed is **quarantined after a bounded number of attempts** (transcript preserved) instead of re-triggering recovery on every boot.

## Changes

- **`SessionEntry`** gains `restartRecoveryAttempts`, `restartRecoveryQuarantinedAt`, `restartRecoveryQuarantineReason` (all optional, backwards compatible).
- **Mark phase** (`markRestartAbortedMainSessions`, the startup-orphan scan, the from-locks scan): increment `restartRecoveryAttempts` — but **only on a fresh interruption** (when the entry was not already `abortedLastRun`), so re-marking within one boot does not double-charge the budget.
- **Recovery gate** (`recoverStore`): if `restartRecoveryAttempts > MAX_RECOVERY_RETRIES`, **quarantine** the entry — clear `abortedLastRun`, record `restartRecoveryQuarantinedAt` + reason `exceeded_restart_retry_budget`, WARN log — instead of resuming. The transcript pointer is preserved so the session is not lost; a future user-driven inbound can still use it.
- **Budget reset on progress**: a successful run (`status: "done"`) clears `restartRecoveryAttempts` + quarantine via `derivePersistedSessionLifecyclePatch`, so an unrelated future interruption gets a fresh allowance.

Scoped to the **main-session** recovery surface. The subagent-orphan-recovery path shares the same architecture; that surface is a documented follow-up to keep this diff focused and reviewable.

## Behavioral contract

| Condition | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| Wedged entry, 1st–3rd restart | Re-marked, resumed | Re-marked, resumed (`attempts` 1→3) |
| Same entry, 4th restart | Re-marked, resumed (loop) | **Quarantined**; `abortedLastRun=false`, `quarantinedAt` set, WARN logged |
| Successful run on the entry | n/a | budget + quarantine cleared |

## Evidence

**Behavior addressed:** an entry with `restartRecoveryAttempts: 4` (budget exceeded) must NOT be resumed; it must be quarantined with the transcript preserved.

**Exact command run after the patch:**
```
npx vitest run src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts
```

**Captured output AFTER fix:**
```
Test Files  2 passed (2)
     Tests  137 passed (137)
```

Targeted quarantine assertion:
```
npx vitest run src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts -t "quarantine" --project agents-core
✓ agents-core ../../src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts (38 tests | 37 skipped) 13ms
      Tests  1 passed | 37 skipped (38)
```

**Regression tests (assert the NEW behavior, fail without the fix):**
- `quarantines a wedged session once it exceeds the cross-boot recovery budget` — asserts `recovered:0, skipped:1`, no gateway resume call, `restartRecoveryQuarantineReason === "exceeded_restart_retry_budget"`, transcript pointer preserved.
- `still resumes a wedged session while it is within the recovery budget` — `attempts:3` still resumes (boundary).
- `increments restartRecoveryAttempts when marking a fresh interruption`.
- `does not double-charge the budget when re-marking an already-aborted entry`.
- `clears the restart-recovery budget + quarantine on a successful run` (lifecycle).

**Type/format checks:** `npx tsc --noEmit` clean for the touched files; `oxfmt` applied.

**What was NOT tested:** the subagent-orphan-recovery surface (out of scope, documented follow-up).
